stub: He is a Labour MP who comes from a white middle class background.
constituency: He is from a constituency that is not your constituency.
personalization: He rarely speaks out and votes against his party leadership.
substantive: He supports increasing the basic Universal Credit grant by £20 per week.
justification: In political debates, he regularly uses arguments that appeal to society as a whole.
responsiveness: Asked recently about the role and duties of politicians, he said: “If I lose an election doing what I think is right, then so be it.”
text: He is a Labour MP who comes from a white middle class background. He is from a constituency that is not your constituency. He rarely speaks out and votes against his party leadership. He supports increasing the basic Universal Credit grant by £20 per week. In political debates, he regularly uses arguments that appeal to society as a whole. Asked recently about the role and duties of politicians, he said: “If I lose an election doing what I think is right, then so be it.”
